instruction: Classify the inspection note into a defect category.
baseline_prompt: You are a quality-assurance defect classifier. Classify the inspection note into a defect category. Classify into one of these categories: surface, dimensional, assembly, electrical, material, labeling.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Several hairline abrasions detected under raking light inspection on the polished stainless steel trim piece, compromising the mirror-like finish quality.
Angle between mating faces is 91.2 degrees, tolerance band is 90.0° ±0.5°
Protective foam insert left inside compartment during final assembly. Blocks access to service port.
LED indicator remains dark when unit is powered on; traced issue to broken solder joint on resistor R24 in the indicator circuit path.
The adhesive supplied has a viscosity and cure time inconsistent with the epoxy resin system specified. MSDS review confirms it is an acrylic-based product instead.
The expiration date stamp on the packaging shows 2019 but this batch was manufactured in 2024 according to production records.
The brushed metal texture is irregular with swirl marks visible in the center section, inconsistent with the specified uniform grain pattern.
Caliper reading shows plate thickness of 3.78mm when drawing specifies 4.00mm ±0.10mm, below minimum acceptable
Rubber gasket omitted from valve assembly. Seal surface completely bare with no elastomer present.
Oscilloscope shows excessive ripple voltage of 850mV peak-to-peak on DC rail; filtering appears inadequate and noise exceeds 100mV specification limit.
Visual inspection reveals the wood grain pattern and density are inconsistent with the mahogany veneer specified in the purchase order. Appears to be a different species entirely.
Bilingual instruction tags are only printed in English; French text is missing despite Canadian market requirements.
Chrome plating exhibits tarnishing and minor pitting along the edges, with loss of luster compared to specification sample provided by engineering.
Thread pitch gauge indicates 1.25mm instead of required 1.0mm pitch on M10 fastener hole
Front panel bezel not snapped into all retention clips. Right side protrudes 3mm from flush position while left side properly seated.

test.query: Continuity test failed on pin 7 to ground connection; multimeter shows open circuit where specification requires less than 0.5 ohms resistance.
Incoming inspection detected that the metal sheets have a galvanized finish rather than the cold-rolled steel specified. This will affect subsequent welding operations.
Warning decal regarding voltage requirements is completely absent from the rear panel where it should be located per safety standards.
Small pit marks and bubbling present in the painted surface near the mounting bracket, suggesting contamination during application process.
Bore diameter came in at 25.45mm, needs to be 25.00mm with +0.02/-0.00 tolerance per blueprint
Left wheel attached backwards with tread pattern facing wrong direction. Vehicle pulls slightly during operation.
Relay coil does not energize when control signal applied; measured coil resistance of 2.1 megohms indicates open winding where 120 ohms expected.
The component appears to be fabricated from a lower-grade alloy than specified in the engineering drawing. Hardness testing confirms values below the required threshold for 316 stainless steel.
Size indicator on the garment tag states XL but the actual measurements correspond to a medium according to the size chart.
The anodized finish shows discoloration and uneven patches across approximately 30% of the visible exterior, particularly concentrated near welded joints.
Wall thickness measured at 2.1mm in three locations, minimum required is 2.5mm per engineering standard
Heat sink mounted without thermal paste application between CPU and cooling surface. Direct metal-to-metal contact observed.
Short circuit detected between phase conductors during hi-pot testing at 1500V; insulation breakdown occurred in the transformer winding section.
Tensile strength testing shows the wire fails at 180 MPa, well below the 250 MPa minimum for Grade A copper. Chemical analysis suggests impurities exceed acceptable limits.
The product identification sticker shows an incorrect part number that doesn't match the manufacturing specifications or the purchase order documentation.
Powder coating appears thin and inconsistent, with several areas showing a mottled appearance and reduced coverage allowing substrate to show through.
CMM inspection reveals 0.35mm deviation from true position on bolt circle, exceeds GD&T callout of 0.20mm
Screw missing from bottom left corner of housing unit. Three mounting points secured but fourth hole empty.
Insulation resistance test between chassis and live conductors reads 0.8 megohms; requirement specifies minimum 2 megohms for safety compliance.
The plastic housing exhibits stress cracking under standard load conditions, indicating use of recycled or degraded polycarbonate instead of virgin material as contracted.
QR code sticker is placed upside down on the bottom of the unit, making it difficult to scan during inventory processing.
The protective clear coat exhibits orange peel texture in several areas near the handle grip, affecting the smoothness of the finish.
Measured shaft diameter at 15.23mm, specification calls for 15.00mm ±0.15mm, exceeds upper tolerance limit by 0.08mm
Two identical components installed where specification calls for mirror-image pair. Both marked as right-hand version.
